question: Is meta-lesson fraction growing monotonically as T5 predicts? Can it drop below 20% without structural cap?
method: Measured meta-domain fraction across 1253 lessons by 100-lesson windows and 50-session blocks. Compared stock (cumulative) vs flow (recent production rate). Checked productivity (L/session) across phases.
prediction: T5: meta fraction grows monotonically without external cap; >=29% at S561
diff: T5 monotonic growth prediction FALSIFIED — meta fraction oscillates, not monotonic. Recent production rate (13.5-28.1%) is well below 29% prediction WITHOUT any structural cap. Expert dispatch (F-EXP7, ~S440+) acts as organic structural constraint by routing work to non-meta domains.
effect_size: -29.8pp phase shift (57.9% -> 28.1% meta) between S450-S499 and S500-S527
productivity_impact: +61.6% (3.28 -> 5.30 L/session) — productivity INCREASED while meta dropped
verdict: T5 monotonic growth: FALSIFIED. F-EPIS4 falsification condition (meta <20% + productivity maintained) is MET in flow rate but NOT in stock. Cumulative requires ~200 more non-meta lessons to cross 20%.
mechanism: Expert dispatch (F-EXP7) is the organic structural constraint that T5 said didn't exist. Domain-routing naturally reduces meta fraction without explicit caps.
